🎯 Company-Specific KPIs
Custom · Built with Zach
The KPIs above are the universal F.O.S.M. metrics that matter for any business. This section is reserved for the KPIs that matter specifically for — — your industry, your business model, your moat. We'll co-author this with Zach in a dedicated session and customize it to surface what's most leveraged for your specific company.
Likely candidates for —: AI sales-tech competitive moat metrics, subcontractor capacity per consultant, recurring vs. one-shot revenue mix, churn cohorts by lead source, retention by consultant, and other industry-tuned indicators.
To-Do List ✨ Live sync — included in monthly service

F.O.S.M. Pillar Trust Scores
Items Needing Attention
Numbers flagged as stale or unverified. Each item shows the recommended fix.
Data Sources
Integrations that pull authoritative data into your Command Center.
Audit Coverage by Source
How many KPIs are audited against each source.
How to Read the Dots
Audited — verified + human confirmed
Verified — fresh from ledger
Stale — verified but >60 days old
Unverified — manual entry, no audit trail
Missing — slot has no value yet
Inferred — AI working assumption
Hover any dot anywhere in the platform to see the data's source, as-of date, and audit status.

🎯 Company-Specific Analysis
Custom-built for —. The variables and recommendations on this page are tuned to your industry and business model.
Status: Awaiting Custom Build
This page is a placeholder for a custom analysis built specifically for —. Unlike the generic F.O.S.M. dashboards (which work for any business), this page surfaces the variables, levers, and recommendations that matter for your specific industry — — — and your specific business model.
Expected to include items like: AI-tooling competitive moat metrics, subcontractor economics modeling, recurring vs one-shot revenue mix targets, churn cohort dynamics specific to AI-calling SaaS, capacity planning across the Philippines consultant team, and industry-specific multiples for valuation. The exact composition is built collaboratively with Zach in a dedicated session.
Variables We'll Tune
  • Industry-specific KPIs (AI sales-tech benchmarks)
  • Custom unit economics formulas (your subcontractor model)
  • Bespoke conversion funnel weights
  • Industry-specific churn cohort math
  • Custom valuation multiples by stage
Recommendations We'll Surface
  • Industry-specific quick wins
  • Competitive intelligence vs AI sales-tech peers
  • Custom risk/opportunity flags
  • Bespoke playbooks (Customer Success for AI SaaS, etc.)
  • Industry-tuned scenario boundaries
📅 NEXT STEP
In your next dedicated session with Zach, we'll co-author this page. Bring: (1) any industry reports or competitor data you've collected, (2) specific decisions you're stuck on, (3) variables you want tracked that aren't in the generic dashboards. The output of that session replaces this placeholder with your actual custom analysis.

Family Office
Where personal wealth gets protected, multiplied, and transferred — beyond what your CPA covers
🏛️ What is your Family Office?
Your business is creating wealth. The Family Office is how you protect, multiply, and transfer it. At $1M revenue you can manage this with a CPA and a will. At $10M, the same setup quietly costs you 6-figure mistakes — in taxes you didn't know you were paying, in estate friction your family inherits, in liabilities the business is silently exposed to.

Below: the full universe of what a Family Office handles. Below that: what we recommend you focus on now, and the impact of acting vs. not acting. We share impact. You decide.
📐 The Universe — What Family Offices Handle 12 categories, organized by maturity tier
🎯 What To Focus On Now AI-generated recommendations based on your stage
Each recommendation shows the impact of acting vs. not acting, with cost/upside/risk concretely framed. We make the recommendation. You make the decision.
Analyzing your stage and computing prioritized recommendations with impact framing...
Click ⟳ AI Recommendation Scan above for prioritized recommendations on what to focus on now — with cost, upside, risk-mitigation, and time-to-implement for each.
🏛️ The Wealth Cockpit Build your wall, brick by brick — with live signals from across your app
Your Money Brick Wall below shows the maturity of every category in one visual. Click a brick to jump to its panel. Each panel pulls live signals from elsewhere in your app — revenue exposure on Estate Planning, entity values on Tax Structure, last-reviewed stamps on Reporting. As you fill in notes, advisors, and review dates, the wall builds itself.

When the foundation is solid, generate the Executor's Briefing at the bottom — the single document your spouse or successor needs if something happens to you tomorrow.
📂 The Twelve Panels Click any header to expand · status, notes, advisors, AI deep-dive
📄 Executor's Briefing
A single printable document compiling everything documented above — the file your spouse or successor reads if something happens to you tomorrow. Generate, then use Print → Save as PDF.

💵 Cash Flow
Indirect-method cash flow — derived from Income Statement + Balance Sheet movements until QBO Cash Flow report is uploaded.
💧 Cash Bridge — Latest Month
Beginning cash + sources/uses = Ending cash
Each bar shows a cash movement. Green = sources of cash · Red = uses of cash · Blue = beginning + ending positions. Movements reconcile exactly to the balance-sheet cash delta.
📈 Cash Position Over Time
7-month trajectory
Solid line: cash balance from balance sheet · Bars below: monthly net Δ cash (green = inflow, red = outflow).
📋 Cash Flow Statement (Indirect Method)
Click any cell for the derivation formula
Cells with the ▣ dot are derived from Income + Balance movements. They will be replaced with raw values once QBO Cash Flow Statement is uploaded.
The lever we'd pull: Click "AI Analyze" below to generate a context-aware operations insight from your current KPI state.
Operations is a yes/no fulfillment funnel. The ☑ rows measure the % of customers who pass each gate: did they get onboarded on time? did they activate? did they hit first milestone? did they renew? Each gate is a leverage point. Hover ⓘ for definitions.
Operations KPIs

Deep Dive — Operations
Generate an AI deep dive on your Operations KPIs — 3 key signals, 2 risks, 2 recommendations.
The lever we'd pull: Click "AI Analyze" below to generate a context-aware sales insight from your current KPI state.
Sales is a yes/no acquisition funnel. The ☑ rows measure each gate: did they show? did they qualify? did they close? did they hand off cleanly? Below the funnel: deal economics, unit economics (CAC/LTV), and team performance.
Sales KPIs
March 2026
KPIFebMarApr ProjTrendingOn TrackRYGFormulaDescription
Deep Dive — Sales
Generate an AI deep dive on your Sales KPIs — 3 key signals, 2 risks, 2 recommendations.
The lever we'd pull: Click "AI Analyze" below to generate a context-aware marketing insight from your current KPI state.
Marketing is tracked by channel. Every lead source has its own funnel: Spend → Leads → Bookings → Cost-per-stage. Top-of-funnel totals first, then each channel broken out. Channel mix tells you where to push more budget and where to fix a leak. Channels with [ELICIT] need confirmation from Zach — he may run cold outreach (the Platform on himself?) or pure paid. Hover the ⓘ icon for definitions.

◎ Vision To Reality
Define the monthly income you need to live the life you actually want. Talk to Zach AI below — describe your perfect day, your adventures, your obligations. AI researches real prices and builds the tables for you.
Your Dream Life Number
Dream Day
$0
/ month
+
Adventure List
$0
/ month (amortized)
+
Monthly Budget
$0
/ month
=
Dream Life Number
$0
/ month income needed
Dream Day is what you spend on a typical day — recurring lifestyle. Adventure List is bucket-list items (trips, big purchases) divided across the months you'll save for them. Monthly Budget is your fixed obligations (housing, family, savings target). The total is the income you need every month to live this life — exactly the way you want.

Client Overview
The source of truth — every industry comparison and report downstream reads from here
🏢 Identity & Tax Who you are · how you're structured · where you're taxed
Company Name 📥 from Tax Returns when wired
Owner Name 📥 from QuickBooks when wired
Entity Type
Year Founded
State / Country of Incorporation
Owner Tax Residency 📥 from Tax Returns when wired
EIN / Business Number
Primary Business Activity drives industry classification ↓
This text feeds the AI for industry classification (next section) and downstream comparisons. Be specific.
Founder Notes audit input ↓
Qualitative observations about the founder — what they're great at, where they get stuck. Feeds the Leadership pillar of the audit.
Aspirations / Dream Life Number audit input ↓
Long-arc goals. Feeds the Leadership pillar + the close pitch (right-sizing fee tier to ambition).
Key Concerns audit input ↓
The actual pain. Feeds opportunity prioritization — these concerns get extra weight in audit ranking.
👥 Additional Entities Other LLCs, trusts, holding companies — anything you also own or operate
🏭 Industry Classification — SIC + NAICS Codes that drive every industry comparable, benchmark, and report
Pick the codes that actually represent your business. AI suggests two buckets — highly recommended (strong fit) and similar but not 100% (worth considering). Check what fits, skip what doesn't, add manual codes if you have specifics. The "in use" pills at the top are what every downstream Industry Compare and Industry Report query reads from.
Click ⟳ Generate Recommendations to have AI propose codes based on your Primary Business Activity above.
Researching SIC and NAICS codes for your business...
⚔️ Competitors Real peer companies — research, review, pick 10
AI proposes 6 real competitors per batch using your active SIC/NAICS codes + business activity from above. Each card shows website, social links, ICP, estimated AOV, company size, revenue range, plus "why this is your competitor" and "why they'd make a good case study." Click ✅ Yes to add to your final list (target: 10), ❌ No to skip (your reasons train the next batch). Pool auto-refills when low.
Researching real companies via web search... this can take 30-50 seconds.
🏆 Leaders Best-in-class — the people you're trying to become, not your peers
AI proposes 6 real industry leaders per batch — established, scaled companies whose marketing, content, or strategy is best-in-class. Each card shows website, socials, ICP, estimated AOV/size/revenue, "why they're a leader," and what to specifically learn from them. Pick 4 to study and emulate. Yes/No with reasons trains the next batch.
Researching real industry leaders via web search... this can take 15-25 seconds.
👥 Clients
Manage every client's separate workspace — switch between them, add new ones, edit names
Each client gets a fully isolated workspace — separate company profile, industry codes, competitors, leaders, family office state, industry compare data, and reports. Switching the active client (here or via the header dropdown) reloads the entire app with that client's data. Zach is seeded as the default client.
+ Add a new client
New clients start with a fresh, empty workspace.
⬆ Import a client config
Drop a previously exported .json config file to restore a client (e.g. backups, demo configs from prospects' free audits).
📦 Install a new client from audit bundle
Audit teams produce a JSON bundle after the F.O.S.M. Audit. Paste or upload below to configure the template for that client. This replaces the active client's identity + seeded prose (live ledger data is unchanged — that's wired separately).
No file selected.
All Clients

These edits modify the active client's data only. Switch clients above to edit a different one.

Bridge the gap between quarters. Quarter blocks pull live from Quarter Goals (read-only here). Each month between quarters has F.O.S.M. goals — click ✨ AI Generate to draft them based on the surrounding quarter trajectories. Expand the weekly section in any month to plan week-by-week.

Daily & Weekly Rhythm
What to focus on right now — pulled from everything the system knows. Monthly milestones live on the F.O.S.M. Planner page; quarterly rocks live on Quarter Goals.
📋 Today —
Based on your ledger, audit findings, calendar, and what's stuck — here are today's recommended priorities in priority order. Snooze to bump to tomorrow, dismiss if you disagree, mark done as you ship.
Reading your data and prioritizing...
Loading today's priorities...
📊 This Week —
Week-level priorities — bigger arcs than today's tasks. Build Critical Path generates a sequenced plan that bridges your current state to where you need to be by end of week, ordered by what unblocks what.
🛤 Build Critical Path — bridge from where you are to where you need to be
Tell the AI what you want to be true by the end of the week, and it will return a sequenced plan — what unblocks what, what depends on what, what to do first.
Tip: be specific. "Reduce churn" is too vague. "Have a churn-cause hypothesis ready to test, with 3 churned clients interviewed" is a real target.

Industry Intelligence
Two industries shape your future. The one your clients are in, and the one you're in.
Why downstream-first
Your clients' industry tells you what's coming for you, often 60-180 days before it shows up in your own market. If your clients are realtors and AI is replacing real-estate agents, you don't have to wait for your revenue to dip to know it's coming — you can pivot. Read the downstream first. Then read your own. Then look at both together to figure out where you need to be moving.
🔭 Downstream — clients' industry
Loading...
🎯 Your industry
Loading...
⚙ ICP Definition
These two descriptions are what the AI uses to scan each industry. The more specific you make them — segment, revenue band, pain point, geography — the more useful the dashboard. Edits save locally.
🔭 Industry your clients are in (downstream)
🎯 Industry you're in (self)
🔭 Industry your clients are in
Forward-looking lens. What's happening to your customers' market — across Finance / Operations / Sales / Marketing — tells you what's coming for yours.
Searching the web (15-22s typical)...
Click ⟳ AI Refresh to scan the web for current trends in your clients' industry.
🎯 Your industry
Immediate lens. What's happening in your market right now — competitive dynamics, M&A, pricing, regulation, AI disruption.
Searching the web (15-22s typical)...
Click ⟳ AI Refresh to scan the web for current trends in your own industry.
🧭 What This Means For Us
Connects downstream signals to your own-industry dynamics and produces a single set of time-horizoned moves. Run both lens scans above first, then click ⟳ Run Synthesis.
Connecting the dots between both lenses...
Run both lens scans above first. The button activates once both have data.
Economic Dashboard
Read the global economy at a glance — drill into countries, industries, and sectors. Everything connects to everything; this is the picture.
🎯 What This Means For Your Business
The connective tissue. Pulls signals from Country Drilldown, Macro Indicators, and Industry Temperature for United States, runs them against your business context, and outputs 3-5 concrete moves tagged by category and time horizon. No web calls — synthesizes from cached data above.
Connecting the dots (~10-15s)...
Run at least 2 of 3 upstream modules first (Country Drilldown · Macro Indicators · Industry Temperature), then click ⟳ Synthesize to generate client-specific actions.
🌍 Global Temperature Check
Each country is colored by overall economic health. Green = expanding · Yellow = mixed/slowing · Red = contracting. Click any country for the full drilldown.
Reading the global economy (15-22s)...
Click ⟳ AI Refresh above to scan global economic health and color the map.
🇺🇸 United States — Country Drilldown
Narrative · macro indicators · stock indices · real estate · consumer financial health for the United States. Click a country on the map above to switch the drilldown · hover any tile for an educational explainer.
Building the country dashboard (~15-22s)...
Click ⟳ AI Refresh to load the full country drilldown — narrative + macro indicators + stock indices + industry temperature grid + consumer financial health.
📊 Macro Indicators — United States
Eight key indicators with 12-month trends. Green sparkline = improving trajectory · Red = deteriorating · Gray = neutral. Hover any tile for the educational explainer + business implication.
Reading the macro picture (~15-22s)...
Click ⟳ AI Refresh to scan macro indicators for the United States — fed rate, inflation, unemployment, GDP, consumer confidence, manufacturing PMI, housing starts, M2 money supply.
🏭 Industry Temperature — United States
12 industries colored by current health. Green = expanding · Yellow = mixed/slowing · Red = contracting. Click any industry to see top performers, hiring trend, capex trend, and notable signals.
Reading industry temperatures (~15-22s)...
Click ⟳ AI Refresh to scan 12 industries for the United States — Tech, Manufacturing, Financial Services, Healthcare, Real Estate, Energy, Retail, Consumer Discretionary, Construction, Transportation, Hospitality, Telecom.
Company Valuation
What this business is worth today, and what each fix is worth at exit. Inputs pull live from the ledger; multiples show low–high ranges; AI calibrates bands to your industry + stage.
Financial Inputs
Pulled from the ledger as trailing-12-month (TTM) totals. Edit any cell to override; ⟳ resets that field to ledger.
Estimated Value Range (low / mid / high across methods)
Conservative
—
→
Base Case

→

✓ Toward the High End
✗ Toward the Low End
What Each Improvement Is Worth
Improvement
Multiple Impact
Valuation Impact
Total Achievable Uplift +$2.5M to +$2.9M
Recent M&A Comparables
Coming in Batch 3b: real comparables with transaction dates + source links via web search.
TargetSegmentRevenueMultipleBuyer / Notes
The valuation playbook: If the goal is sale or strategic exit in 3–5 years, the work starts today. The difference between a 4× and a 7× multiple on $1.5M of EBITDA is $4.5M — worth three to five years of deliberate system-building. Customer diversification, margin validation, TTM history, documented operations, proper working-capital financing. Every monthly intelligence report tracks progress toward the higher end of the defensible range.

🏖️
The L&GR Retreat
An invitation-only gathering of founders building businesses that work for all. Five days of clarity, community, and the work that makes everything else possible.
Next Retreat
[ELICIT — Date & Location]
Five days. Twelve founders. One purpose: peace AND prosperity.
What It Is
Five days designed to dismantle the gap between what your business is doing and what your life actually needs it to do. Vision work, F.O.S.M. recalibration, and the deep human work most founders never make time for.
Why It Matters
Most founders are running businesses that look successful from outside but feel hollow from inside. Prosperity without peace isn't the goal. The Retreat is where founders rebuild that integration — with their numbers, their team, and themselves.
Who It's For
Founders running businesses between $1M and $20M in revenue who feel the gap between what they've built and what they actually want. People who care about peace as much as prosperity. People building businesses that work for all.
The Five Days
Day 1
Arrival & The Truth
Where you actually are. The numbers behind the story. The story behind the numbers.
Day 2
Vision To Reality
Where you're going. What "won" looks like. The Dream Life Number, defined.
Day 3
F.O.S.M. Reset
The four pillars examined honestly. Which one is starving. Which one is running you.
Day 4
The Plan
Twelve months written down. Specific. Sequenced. Owned by the right people.
Day 5
Integration & Return
Bringing it home. The first thirty days. The community that holds you to it.
What's Included
  • Five days, four nights — accommodations included
  • All meals — chef-prepared, intentional, restorative
  • One-on-one strategic time with Zach
  • Group sessions with eleven other founders
  • Custom F.O.S.M. workbook for your business
  • 12-month follow-up integration with the L&GR community
What You Leave With
  • A written one-page Vision To Reality for your business
  • Your Dream Life Number, defined with rigor
  • A 12-month F.O.S.M. plan with named owners
  • An honest read of where each pillar is weak
  • Eleven peer founders who become your board
  • Renewed clarity about why this matters
Investment
[ELICIT — Tuition] per founder. A portion of every retreat fee funds the L&GR Foundation directly — children in 47 countries are educated in part because founders show up here. Prosperity AND purpose, built into the price.
📅 NEXT STEP
The Retreat is invitation-only. The application is short — twenty minutes — and it's how we make sure the room is the right room. If this resonates, request an invitation and Zach will be in touch within five business days.
Important Links
Quick access to all essential tools
Add Custom Link
🗺 Architecture Map
The single source-of-truth diagram of how the entire app thinks. Every page, every data source, every dependency — tracked. This is the audit reference: when we update a value on one page, this map tells us every downstream consumer that needs to recalculate. Maintained as architecture evolves.
—
Pages tracked
—
Data sources
—
Read flows mapped
—
Write flows mapped
📓 Data Spine — Source of Truth at the center
At the center: Source of Truth (the ledger). Around it: data structures grouped by category. Each category lists its data sources with the count of pages that read from each. The principle: numbers are entered in one place and consumed everywhere. When this map shows a tight ring around a single ledger, integrity is high. When sources duplicate, integrity drifts.
📄 Page Registry — every page, what it reads and writes
For every page in the app: which data sources it consumes (reads) and which it produces (writes). Use this to trace: "if I change X, what pages need to re-render?" — find the data source in the writes column, then look up its consumers in the data-source registry below.
🔗 Data Source Registry — every data store, its consumers and producers
For every data source: who reads from it, who writes to it. Use this to verify single source of truth — every data source should have at most one producer (the page where data is entered or computed). Multiple producers = duplication = integrity risk.
Architecture map is a static registry of intent — kept manually current as features ship. Discrepancies between this map and runtime behavior are integrity bugs. Keep this map honest.
⚙ Settings & Admin
Deliberate operational actions for this client's platform. These are infrequent but consequential — read each card carefully before acting. All actions affect the active client only.
📅 Roll Forward to Next Month
Advances the platform's current month anchor. Use this when you've closed accounting for the current month and want the platform to start tracking the next one. Choose empty for a clean roll, or with P&L actuals to import a QBO Profit & Loss for the closing month at the same time.
↩ Reverse Roll Forward
Symmetric undo of the most recent roll-forward. Moves the current month anchor back by 1 and shrinks the projection horizon by 1.
Cell data is preserved. If you imported actuals into the now-future month, the values stay in their cells. They just stop being "current" — you'll see them again if you roll forward. To clear data, edit cells manually on the Source of Truth page.
📊 Upload Balance Sheet
Imports a QBO Balance Sheet for the selected period. Balance Sheet is point-in-time — values are end-of-period snapshots, not flows. This card does NOT roll the timeline; use the Roll Forward card separately when you want to advance the current-month anchor.
🔒 Lock / Unlock Month
Closes a month for further data-import changes by flagging it as locked. This is a structural lock only — cell values stay as-is, and per-cell audit flags are unchanged. After locking, you do the visual audit pass on the Source of Truth page (eye-check each value against QuickBooks, click each cell to mark audited).
Lock eligibility: any month on or before — that isn't already locked. Lock is reversible — see the Unlock section below.
More admin actions coming soon
Planned: Year close / archive prior year, Import client bundle, Export client bundle, Recompute caches.
🔍 Timeline State (read-only)
Current state of the timeline source-of-truth for this client. Updates when you roll forward or lock a month.

Ask Zach
AI advisor using F.O.S.M. Framework with your full business context
⚙️ Money Making Machine
The full mechanics-of-money flow: change ONE funnel input → watch revenue, expenses, gross profit, net income, cash flow, AND the balance sheet all cascade. Three columns: Feb actual / Mar actual / Apr scenario.
Project April Using:
Choose which historical actuals form the baseline for April's projection. Funnel inputs come from this baseline; existing-book projection compounds from Mar's existing revenue.
April Projected Net Income

vs. March
—

—
What's Driving It
Click any knob below to test a scenario.
📢 The Funnel — Marketing → Sales → Revenue
This is what produces new-sales revenue. Each stage flows into the next. Adjust knobs below to change the flow.
⚙️ The Mechanics of Money — How Revenue Becomes Cash & Equity
From the Finance Department playbook (page 4). New-sales revenue (from the funnel above) plus existing book feeds into the Income Statement. Net Income flows to the Cash Flow Statement. The cash change moves the Balance Sheet. Adjust a knob and watch every box update.
▶ Playing Field (P&L) ▶ Money Movement (Cash Flow) ▶ Scoreboard (Balance Sheet)
Adjustable Variables (April Scenario)
Click a field to make it active. Editing one variable locks the others to baseline. Watch all three financial statements update.
📊 Income Statement
How profit is generated
Revenue minus all costs equals Net Income. Net Income flows into the Cash Flow Statement.
Line Item Feb Actual Mar Actual Apr Scenario Δ vs Mar
💵 Cash Flow Statement
How profit becomes cash
Starts with Net Income. Adjusts for non-cash items, working-capital changes, investing, and financing. The result moves cash on the Balance Sheet.
Line Item Feb Actual Mar Actual Apr Scenario Δ vs Mar
⚖️ Balance Sheet
What's on the books
End-of-month snapshot. Cash moves with cash flow. Equity = prior equity + Net Income (the closing identity that makes the whole thing tie out).
Line Item Feb Actual Mar Actual Apr Scenario Δ vs Mar
Triangle of Death
Are you profitable on every sale? Define what you sell, who you sell it to, and how much you sell it for — then test it against the true cost to deliver. Most companies are giving too much away.
①WHAT You Sell
✨ AI
Your product or service. Be specific — vague offers kill deals.
②WHO You Sell It To
✨ AI
Your ideal client avatar. Income level, role, where they spend time, what they care about.
③HOW MUCH You Sell It For
✨ AI
Your pricing model. The true profitability check is below — fulfillment cost vs. price.
$
Avatar & Price-Sensitivity Analysis
Who is your buyer really, and what's their relationship to price? Are they in a high-churn industry (realtors, restaurants, fitness) where retention is structurally hard? Are they price-sensitive entry-level buyers who can't afford to stay? This analysis flags structural fits and misfits.
Click ✨ AI Analyze Avatar to generate a price-sensitivity read on your client avatar — income, role, market context, and whether you're in a high-churn category.
True Cost of Fulfillment (per client)
Form-based · chatbot wizard coming soon
Most founders price by what feels right — not by what it costs to deliver. Add line items below for each cost. If your monthly price minus this cost is negative, you're paying customers to be customers.
One-Time Onboarding Cost
Time and costs spent setting up a new client. + Labor pulls a person from your Org Chart — rate auto-fills from their monthly comp ÷ 173 hrs/mo (40 hrs/wk × 52/12). + Flat is a one-time vendor cost.
—
Monthly Fulfillment Cost
Recurring time + costs per client per month. + Labor pulls a person from your Org Chart — rate auto-fills from their monthly comp ÷ 173 hrs/mo. + Flat is a recurring vendor cost (software seat, processing fee, etc.).
—

Are You Giving Too Much Away?
Compares your contribution margin against industry norms for your category. Most founders overdeliver to people who can't afford it. This output will tell you if that's happening — and what to do about it.
Fill in pricing + cost-of-fulfillment above, then click ✨ AI Analyze Pricing for a structured read on whether you're underwater.

Industry Comparison
How your numbers stack against multiple industry benchmark sources — because no single source has the truth. The best-fit source for your business is highlighted green, second-best yellow, based on which study's sample most closely matches your company size and stage.
📊 Multi-Source Benchmark
Each industry study samples differently — some focus on private SMBs, others on public companies, others on a specific revenue band. Comparing across sources reveals the real range. The best-fit source for your specific company is the one to weight most heavily — that's the green column.
Pulling current benchmark data from 4 industry sources...
Click ⟳ AI Refresh above to scan IBISWorld, RMA Annual Statement Studies, NAICS, BizMiner, and similar sources for current benchmark data.
📐 Where You Sit In The Distribution
Every industry has a natural ceiling and floor. Below: revenue, gross profit, and net profit distributions for your industry — with your company's position marked. If you're chasing a goal far above where most operators cap out, you need to see that visually before you burn yourself or your team out chasing it.
Pulling industry distribution data (revenue, gross margin, net margin)...
Click ⟳ AI Distribution Scan above to map where you sit relative to the rest of your industry — by revenue, gross profit, and net profit.
🎯 Ceiling-Break Probability
Growth: %/yr
Knowing where you sit (above) is half the picture. The other half: what's the realistic probability you actually break the ceiling on your current trajectory? Below: pure math + calibrated probability + the friction reasons most operators stall. The gap between the math and the probability is the industry-gravity story.
Computing thresholds, deterministic math, and calibrated probability estimates...
Adjust your growth rate above, then click ⟳ AI Probability Scan to compute realistic ceiling-break probabilities at 1, 3, and 5 year horizons.

🎯 Coaching Center
The inner game: Vision, Emotional Intelligence, Leadership. This is for personal accountability — not P&L analysis. For business numbers go to F.O.S.M. dashboards. Coaching uses your communications + business context to give example-based feedback: "here's what you said, here's what stronger leadership would sound like."
◎ Vision
💡 Emotional Intelligence
👑 Leadership
💬 What would you like coaching on?
Non-binding · No P&L allowed. Bring problems like "my team isn't responsive," "we have culture issues," "people are upset," "I can't get alignment." For finance/ops/sales/marketing tactics, use the F.O.S.M. dashboards instead.
◎ Vision
The anchor everything else coaches against
Your current vision lives here. The workshop below is for refining it together with the AI. When the AI proposes updates you like, click 📥 Apply — the static fields update and EVERY other coaching assessment from then on measures against the new vision.
🛠 Vision Workshop
Talk it out: where are we going? Are we still going there? Want to add or tweak? When the AI proposes updated vision language, the Apply button activates — click it to overwrite the static fields above.
💡 Emotional Intelligence
The AI reads your communications, your current vision, and the client context — then rates you on each EI factor. Click Help me understand my rating on any factor for a deeper breakdown and improvement plan.
Click ✨ AI Rate Me — the AI will assess you on all 5 EI factors with one quoted piece of evidence each. Then click any factor's "Help me understand" button to drill in.
👑 Leadership
The AI rates you across 7 leadership factors — are you inspiring or forcing? enrolling or dominating? Click Help me understand my rating on any factor for the full breakdown and what to do about it.
Click ✨ AI Rate Me — the AI will assess you on all 7 leadership factors with quoted evidence. Then click any factor's "Help me understand" button to drill in.
📥 Your Communications (raw material for the AI)
The more you paste, the sharper the feedback
Drop in recent emails to your team, Slack messages, all-hands talking points, vision statements, 1:1 notes, anything you've sent or said. The AI reads this to assess how clearly your vision is landing, how you're showing up emotionally, and what kind of leader you're being. Nothing leaves the page.

Communications Intelligence
The connective tissue between your numbers and your reality.
What this is
This section reads across your entire data set — emails, Slack threads, meeting transcripts, customer support, sales calls, ClickUp comments, the ledger — and surfaces the patterns you can't see when you're inside the day-to-day. The idea: take all of the signals and turn them into vision for you. The four reports work together:
🔁 Recurring Themes
Patterns showing up across multiple sources. What keeps coming up — in your team meetings, in customer messages, in Slack — that signals something is shifting?
🔍 Said vs. Shown
Where what people say contradicts what the numbers show. The most diagnostic signal — forces the team to confront reality and align on what's actually true.
📣 Client-Side Signals
What clients are actually telling you — praising, silent, frustrated, churning. These signals typically lead revenue changes by 60-90 days.
🌫 What Don't I See?
Blind spots across 10 domains — Economy, Industry, Business, Leadership, Vision, EI, Finance, Ops, Sales, Marketing. We can't see all the data like the system can.
⚙️ Status: Reports run against your CLIENT_CONTEXT, audit findings, and live ledger. The full backend data integration (Gmail, Slack, calendar, CRM) is on the roadmap — once connected, every report below pulls from your actual conversations and inboxes automatically.
🔁 Recurring Themes
Patterns surfacing across your communications. Each theme: what it is, what it likely costs you, how to fix it, and which messages it came from.
Click ⟳ AI Extract Themes. The AI surfaces up to 8 patterns from across your data sources, with cited sources for each.
🔍 Said vs. Shown
Where what people say doesn't match what the numbers show. The AI cites the claim, pulls the actual ledger figure, walks you through its reasoning so you can decide if you agree before acting.
Click ⟳ AI Find Contradictions. Each contradiction shows the exact quote, the ledger reality, and the reasoning chain.
📣 Client-Side Signals
What clients are actually telling you — praising, silent, frustrated, churning. Sentiment grouped, with concrete cleanup suggestions for the relevant area (communication / onboarding / fulfillment / product).
The AI groups client signals by sentiment, ties each to a specific source, and suggests where to clean up.
🌫 What Don't I See?
Blind spots scan · 10 domains
You can't see all your data the way the system can. Pick a domain — the AI surfaces 3-5 things you might be missing in that area, what they could cost you, and how to mitigate. Toggle Enable live web search below if you want current trends pulled in for Economy and Industry (adds latency).
Pick a domain, run the scan, then move to the next. Build the picture one lens at a time.
Scanning...
Pick a domain above and click ⟳ Run Scan. The AI surfaces 3-5 blind spots in that domain — what's potentially missing from your view, why it matters, and how to mitigate.
PART 5
Tax Strategy
What the IRS currently assumes — and what we can do about it.
📋 Business Tax Status
EXAMPLE
👤 Personal Tax Status
Filing status, dependents, brackets, and personal-side context. Drives RRSP/TFSA/IRA strategy, estate planning, and charitable plays.
The first move on tax: File the 2024 T2 return. Reconcile the shareholder loan, A/R, and A/P. Once the books reflect reality and the corporation is current, then we look at SR&ED, RRSP/TFSA, dividends-vs-salary mix, and the long-arc structures. Order of operations matters here.
💼 Business Tax Strategies
Each card shows the strategy, current status, who owns the action, the dollar impact range, and the deadline. Click a strategy that's underlined to jump to the full library entry below.
Estimated annual savings if executed —
👤 Personal Tax Strategies
Personal-side plays — retirement accounts, dividends-vs-salary, charitable giving, capital gains. Driven by your filing status, dependents, and bracket (set in the Personal Tax Status card above).
Estimated annual savings if executed —
⚡ This Month's Priority Tax Actions
The three moves that matter most this month. Sequenced — top first. Each card shows the action, why it's urgent now, and what success looks like (the deliverable).

Personalized recommendations from the 80-strategy library below. AI reads your full client context (entity type, jurisdiction, revenue, stage), live ledger snapshot, and the entire strategy catalog — returns the top 5 strategies most likely to move the needle for YOU, ranked by impact and time-to-deploy. No web calls — synthesizes from cached data.
Reading the catalog and your profile (~10-15s)...
Click ⟳ Find My Top 5 to surface the most-relevant tax strategies for this client from the 80-strategy library below.
📚 US Tax Strategy Library
Comprehensive catalog of US tax strategies — entity structures, retirement wrappers, real estate plays, credits, deductions, and advanced moves. Each entry shows: what it does · whether it costs or saves money · type (deduction / credit / deferral / structure) · estimated savings · whether your money stays under your control · who benefits. Click any strategy to expand. Use filters to narrow down by category, complexity, or fit for your profile.

How much cash is sitting around earning nothing? Total Cash − Minimum Buffer = Deployable. The Minimum Buffer protects your business and personal life — typically 6 months of operating expenses. Anything above that is opportunity cost. Adjust the buffer below.

Business Portfolio —
The observation that matters: Zach's Dream Life Number is $4.5M of investable assets ($15K/month × 25). Currently ~$50K outside the business and ~$77K of book equity inside it. The path: stabilize Test Company profitability per the audit's Q1 plan, then begin systematic transfer of operating profit into RRSP, TFSA, and a corporate investment account. The wealth gets built outside the business once the business stops bleeding inside.
₿ Crypto Opportunities
Current crypto market state + 3-5 strategic plays sized to your deployable capital. Plays — not specific deals — are durable strategies (DCA accumulation, sector rotation, yield harvest) with mechanics, win/lose conditions, and tax angles. Crypto is volatile — typical ceiling is 1-5% of investable assets.
Reading crypto market data (~25-40s)...
Click ⟳ Scan crypto market to read the current crypto landscape and surface 3-5 specific opportunities.
🏘️ Real Estate Opportunities
Current real estate market read + 3-5 strategic plays worth pursuing (cost segregation, REPS, 1031 exchanges, opportunity zones, syndication archetypes). Real estate is the most tax-efficient asset class — most plays link to specific tax library strategies. Each shows market thesis, mechanics, win/lose conditions, tax angle, and sizing.
Reading real estate market trends (~25-40s)...
Click ⟳ Scan real estate market to read current market direction and surface specific opportunities sized to your deployable capital.
📈 Public Markets Opportunities
S&P 500 / total market index as the boring base case that wins, plus 2-3 sector or factor rotation ideas. ETFs and index funds only — no individual stock picks. Includes tax-loss harvesting and direct indexing where relevant.
Reading public market trends (~25-40s)...
Click ⟳ Scan public markets to read current S&P / sector outlook and surface ETF / index strategic plays.
🌟 Alternative Investments
Tax-advantaged plays where tax treatment dominates the return: solar tax credit acquisition (ITC + IDC), oil & gas working interest plays with intangible drilling deductions, private credit yield strategies, farmland REIT exposure. Each play links to specific tax library strategies. Honest about illiquidity, lockups, and accreditation requirements.
Reading alternative investment opportunities (~25-40s)...
Click ⟳ Scan alternative investments to surface tax-advantaged plays in solar, oil & gas, private credit, and farmland.
🎨 Passion Asset Investments
Investments tied to things you actually care about. The wealth-building angle on hobbies — historical cars, watches, art, vintage instruments, baseball cards, fine wine. These are speculative and illiquid, but you'll enjoy the ride. Add your interests below; AI surfaces specific opportunities and current market state.
Your interests
Scanning the passion asset markets (~25-40s)...
Add 1-3 interests above, then click ⟳ Find passion plays.
🎯 Capital Deployment Synthesis
The keystone card. Reads everything above — your deployable capital, the acquisition targets, the asset class scans, your passion interests — and synthesizes a specific deployment plan. Allocation mix across asset classes · top 3 moves to make THIS WEEK · projected portfolio outcome at 5, 10, and 20 years. Fresher upstream scans = sharper recommendations.
Synthesizing across all asset classes (~20-30s)...
Click ⟳ Synthesize deployment plan for an integrated recommendation across all asset classes, prioritized for execution.
💎 Tax-Advantaged Opportunities
Tax-shelter plays appropriate to your jurisdiction and stage. Each card shows the strategy mechanics, eligibility, and the realistic dollar impact for your profile. Use these alongside the strategic plays surfaced by the per-asset-class scans above.
▶ Suggested Next Moves
Three concrete moves to make in sequence. Each is reversible if circumstances change. Bias is toward moves that protect downside before chasing upside.
📍 You Are Here
A single page that answers the founder's Monday-morning question — where am I, and what's next? The math summarizes your numbers (personal life served by business). The message summarizes your story (who you are vs. what your company does). Below: how confident we are in each, what's missing, and whether you're tracking against your Quarter Goals + F.O.S.M. Planner.

PLAN & STRATEGY
Your Board of Directors
Surround yourself with the wisdom of leaders you'd never get in a room with — through the lens of their public work and how they think.
WHAT IS A BOARD?
A group of advisors whose distinct lenses sharpen your decisions. Not a fan club, not a yes-machine — friction is the point. The best boards have heterogeneous thinkers who challenge each other and you.
WHO BELONGS?
Pick people who would tell you something you don't want to hear. Mix lenses — operator who's scaled, financier who's seen distress, builder who plays long, leader who lives well. If everyone agrees, you didn't pick well.
WHAT TO LOOK FOR
Stage-appropriate operators (someone 1-2 stages ahead of you), domain experts (your industry), pattern-recognizers (across industries), and life-design voices (someone who's modeled peace and prosperity, not just success).
SIX DIMENSIONS
Each member rates you on six axes: Math (the accounting), Message (the planning), Life (right reasons), Vision (sharing it with the team), EQ (self-awareness, listening), and Leadership (skills + practice). The numbers matter, but so does the human.
Add a board member. Paste a LinkedIn URL, X profile, or just a public name. AI will research them and build a profile. If there's not enough public information to qualify them as a board member, you'll see a clear "not qualified — insufficient public data" status. Quality over quantity — pick 4-7 who'd each see something different.
CURATED BOARD LIBRARY · 28 profiles, 7 categories
Pick where you're weak, not where you're already strong. Strong at sales? You don't need a sales board member. Pick a category that fills a gap. Click any card to hear that person's pitch — written specifically to your weaknesses.
THE MASTERMIND
Collective Board Report
Your board convenes. Each member brings their lens. They surface what they collectively see — what to focus on, what to develop, what to improve, with concrete suggestions.
Audit auto-population. Reads everything you've populated in the Command Center (CO Profile, ledger, KPIs, tax, investment, team) and generates a complete 7-pillar audit. Every dollar number will cite a specific data source. Click any cited number to see exactly how it was derived.
DATA READINESS
Checking data sources…
F.O.S.M. AUDIT · Test Company 📍 Auditable
$720K/yr of unrealized opportunity
A 7-month review of your books, ops, sales engine, marketing engine, and leadership posture. Five pillars analyzed. Every leak quantified. The plan to fix it is below.
EXECUTION
The 90-day plan
Three months. Sequenced. Each month builds on the last. By Day 90, the leaks are plugged and the engine is rebuilt.
THE MATH
If we capture even half of this, the engagement pays for itself in the first month.
🧠 Context
The AI fills gaps with reasoning when data isn't available. Hard data isn't tracked here — that lives in the ledger. Inferential leaps are. Rules of thumb, market views, personality inferences, jurisdictional guesses, and strategic frames — every claim the AI made beyond what your data proves. Audit each one. Accept, edit, or replace.

📋 Assumption Inbox
Walks every cached AI output across the app — Best Fits · Acquisition Targets · Crypto · Real Estate · Public Markets · Alternatives · Passion · Synthesis · Tax tables — and surfaces the inferential leaps the AI made. Run this whenever you want a fresh sweep or after major AI refreshes. New AI runs going forward will auto-deposit assumptions into this inbox (Session 3).
Walking cached AI outputs and extracting inferential leaps (~30-60s)...
No assumptions tracked yet. Click ⟳ Scan AI outputs for assumptions to extract inferential leaps from your existing AI-generated content. Future AI runs will auto-deposit here.
📋 Verification Report
Each unresolved assumption gets routed to the right owner — the department head who runs that pillar plus Zach (so Zach sees everything across the company). Templated questions by type. Click ✨ Refine on any row to AI-rewrite the question for that role. Export to markdown (clipboard) or PDF (print).
Loading verification report...
Integrity Protocol
Every number in every report traces to a source. Every recommendation ties to a data point or a communication. This is what makes the reporting discipline auditable.

Integrity Statement
Every financial figure in this Command Center traces to a verified source. Every recommendation ties to a source listed above — a specific number from the financials or a specific methodology reference. Integrity checks run before any report is generated; flags are documented, not suppressed.

— The Learn And Grow Rich Team · April 23, 2026
📓 Source of Truth — Master Ledger
Every number in the platform originates here. Income Statement · Balance Sheet · Cash Flow · Marketing · Sales · Operations. Each cell carries an audit state — yellow (unaudited) or green (audited ✓). Click any cell to toggle. Historical months stay locked once the next month is added. Initial seed: F.O.S.M. Audit (Aug 2025 → Mar 2026). Going forward: data flows from QuickBooks + connected sources via Data Connections.
Audit Progress
Loading…
Architecture note: This page is the single source of truth for all data. Other pages (F.O.S.M. KPI tables, Money Making Machine, Quarter Goals, Strategic Plan) currently have their own copies of these numbers. Migration to read from this ledger will happen in subsequent batches. Once migration is complete, this is the only place numbers get edited; everywhere else just displays.
